whenToUse: Use as a generic package-first layout wrapper when axis is abstracted.
Use when generated UI should avoid raw React Native View wrappers.
whenNotToUse: Do not use when row or column intent is explicit; prefer XStack or YStack.

records.summary: Theme-aware text primitive with validated typography variants for package-consistent rendering.
whenToUse: Use for all headings, labels, helper text, and body copy.
Use instead of raw React Native Text in package-first screens.
whenNotToUse: Do not use for non-textual content.

props.name: fontWeight
props.description: Font weight. Accepts any string e.g. '400', '700', 'bold'. Validated.

props.name: color
props.description: Text color as hex (#RRGGBB) or theme color reference. Validated.

props.name: numberOfLines
props.description: Clamps text to N lines with ellipsis.

props.name: selected
props.description: When true applies gray[50] color for selected/highlighted state.

props.name: textDecorationLine

Selected state switches color to theme.colors.gray[50].

bestPractices: Use StyledText as the default text primitive across all screens.
Pass theme color references rather than raw hex strings where possible.
Use numberOfLines for truncation instead of custom ellipsis logic.
antiPatterns: Mixing raw React Native Text and StyledText without a design reason.
Passing invalid textAlign strings — the component throws on invalid values.
generationHints: Prefer StyledText over raw React Native Text.
For dialog titles use fontSize={16} fontWeight='bold' color={theme.colors.gray[900]}.
For dialog body copy use fontSize={14} fontWeight='normal' color={theme.colors.gray[700]}.
Use textDecorationLine={true} as a boolean not a string.

records.summary: Single-line text input with built-in label, error message, border theming, disabled state, and placeholder color. Wraps RN TextInput in a YStack container for consistent vertical layout. Base style uses borderRadius 30, paddingHorizontal 16, paddingVertical 12, gray-50 background, gray-800 text, and theme fontSize.normal.
whenToUse: Use for all short single-line text fields: name, email, search, code, OTP, URL.
Use instead of raw TextInput in any fluent-styles form.
Use the label prop to render a vertically-stacked field label without a separate StyledText.
Use the error + errorMessage props to show inline validation feedback.
whenNotToUse: Do not use for long-form text — use StyledMultiInput instead.
Do not set multiline=true on StyledInput for textarea-like input — use StyledMultiInput which configures multiline, textAlignVertical, and borderRadius correctly.

props.name: label
props.description: Optional label rendered above the input inside a YStack with paddingHorizontal=8. Adds 4px vertical spacers above and below.

props.name: labelProps
props.description: Props spread onto the StyledText label. Use to override color, fontSize, fontWeight, etc.

props.name: containerProps
props.description: Props spread onto the outer YStack container that wraps the input. Controls width, opacity override, custom styles.

props.name: errorMessage
props.description: Error text rendered below the input in red-500. Only rendered when both errorMessage and error=true.

props.name: error
props.description: When true, switches the border color to red-500 and renders the errorMessage if provided.

props.name: errorProps
props.description: Props spread onto the StyledText error message. Use to override color, fontSize, etc.

props.name: borderColor
props.description: Default border color. Overridden by red-500 when error=true.

props.name: multiline
props.description: Passed through to the underlying TextInput. Prefer StyledMultiInput for multiline usage.

props.name: numberOfLines
props.description: Passed through to the underlying TextInput.

props.name: editable
props.description: When false, applies 0.6 opacity to the container and disables text editing.

props.name: placeholder
props.description: Placeholder text. placeholderTextColor is automatically set to theme.colors.gray[400].

variants.name: default
variants.description: Gray-200 border, gray-50 background, gray-800 text, borderRadius 30, px=16, py=12, fontSize=normal.

variants.name: error
variants.description: Border switches to red-500. Error message shown below in red-500 fontSize.small fontWeight 600.

variants.name: disabled
variants.description: Container opacity drops to 0.6 when editable=false. Input still renders but is non-interactive.

notes: All colors sourced from theme.colors. Font sizes from theme.fontSize.
placeholderTextColor is hardcoded to theme.colors.gray[400] — not overridable via props.

bestPractices: Always use the label prop instead of placing a separate StyledText above the input — it handles spacers automatically.
Pass error + errorMessage together — errorMessage alone renders nothing.
Use containerProps to apply custom width, style overrides on the wrapper rather than hacking the input directly.
Pair StyledInput with StyledSpacer or YStack gap for multi-field form layouts.
Use editable={false} for read-only display fields rather than disabling the wrapper.
antiPatterns: Using raw TextInput alongside StyledInput in the same form — breaks visual consistency.
Setting multiline=true on StyledInput — use StyledMultiInput which sets borderRadius 16, textAlignVertical, and numberOfLines correctly.
Passing error=true without errorMessage — the border turns red but no feedback text appears.
Overriding placeholderTextColor in style — it is set internally and cannot be overridden via the style prop.
generationHints: Always use StyledInput over raw TextInput for package-first forms.
Use the label prop — it adds required spacers automatically and keeps form field layout consistent.
Pass error={!!errors.fieldName} and errorMessage={errors.fieldName?.message} for react-hook-form integration.
For read-only fields set editable={false} — do not use a StyledText in place of a disabled input.
For multi-line text areas (notes, comments, descriptions) always switch to StyledMultiInput.
Wrap multiple StyledInput fields in YStack with gap={12} or gap={16} for vertically spaced form layouts.

records.summary: Multi-line text area input with built-in label, error message, border theming, and disabled state. Internally forces multiline=true, textAlignVertical='top', and inputMode='text'. Uses borderRadius 16 (softer than StyledInput's 30) and defaults to 5 visible lines. Use for notes, descriptions, comments, and any long-form free text.
whenToUse: Use for notes, comments, descriptions, bios, or any free-form text expected to span multiple lines.
Use when the number of visible lines needs to be controlled via numberOfLines.
Use the label prop for a vertically-stacked field label without extra markup.
Use the error + errorMessage props for inline validation feedback.
whenNotToUse: Do not use for short single-line fields — use StyledInput which has the correct borderRadius 30 and single-line defaults.
Do not pass multiline or textAlignVertical as props — they are fixed internally.
Do not use for rich text or markdown editing — it is a plain TextInput.

bestPractices: Always use StyledMultiInput instead of StyledInput with multiline=true — it sets borderRadius, textAlignVertical, and inputMode correctly.
Use the label prop instead of a separate StyledText — it handles spacers automatically.
Set numberOfLines to match expected content height — default 5 is appropriate for short notes; use 8+ for longer descriptions.
Pass error + errorMessage together — errorMessage alone renders nothing.
Use containerProps to pass custom style overrides on the wrapper YStack.
antiPatterns: Using StyledInput with multiline=true — it keeps borderRadius 30 and does not set textAlignVertical or inputMode.
Using a raw multiline TextInput alongside StyledMultiInput in the same form — breaks visual consistency.
Passing textAlignVertical or inputMode as props — they are set internally and will be overridden.
Setting numberOfLines=1 on StyledMultiInput — use StyledInput for single-line fields.
generationHints: Use StyledMultiInput for any text field where content spans multiple lines — notes, descriptions, comments, bios.
Use the label prop — it adds required spacers and keeps field layout consistent with StyledInput.
Pass error={!!errors.field} and errorMessage={errors.field?.message} for react-hook-form integration.
Set numberOfLines based on expected content: 4-5 for short notes, 6-8 for medium descriptions, 10+ for long content.
Do not use for single-line fields — borderRadius 16 and vertical text alignment will look incorrect.

records.summary: Surface container for grouped content with optional shadow levels and pressable support.
whenToUse: Use for settings groups, profile panels, dashboard tiles, and bounded content blocks.
Use pressable=true with pressableProps when the card must respond to tap.
whenNotToUse: Do not use for modal workflows; prefer StyledDialog or StyledConfirmDialog.
Do not wrap every small inline element in a card.

bestPractices: Use YStack inside StyledCard for internal spacing.
Prefer shadow='medium' for standard tiles.
Use pressable=true only when the entire card is interactive.

Use pressable=true with pressableProps for tappable list tiles.

records.summary: Confirm/cancel dialog with optional neutral button for destructive or consequential actions.

whenNotToUse: Do not use for simple acknowledgement; prefer StyledOkDialog.

bestPractices: Use direct action language for labels: Delete, Remove, Discard instead of generic OK.
Pass row data when the dialog is triggered from a list item.

records.embeddingText: StyledConfirmDialog is the fluent-styles confirm/cancel dialog. Required props: title, description. Key props: cancelLabel (default 'Cancel', red button), confirmLabel (default 'Confirm', green button), neutralLabel (default 'Neutral', orange, shown when showNeutral=true), row (passed to onConfirm), onCancel, onConfirm, onNeutral. Dialog auto-closes on any button press. Use for delete, discard, and irreversible actions.

whenNotToUse: Do not use for confirm/cancel decisions; prefer StyledConfirmDialog.

records.summary: Theme-aware vertical scroll container with padding variants, scroll indicator control, and background color theming. Use as the primary scrollable screen wrapper. Also exports HorizontalScrollView for carousel and horizontal list scenarios.
whenToUse: Use as the root scrollable container for full-screen content views.
Use when content may exceed the visible viewport and needs vertical scrolling.
Use HorizontalScrollView for carousels, image rows, or horizontal chip groups.
Use the padded prop to apply consistent screen-edge spacing without extra wrappers.
whenNotToUse: Do not nest StyledScrollView inside another ScrollView — use FlatList or SectionList for long virtualized lists.
Do not use when content fits in the viewport without scrolling; prefer YStack instead.
Do not use HorizontalScrollView for large lists; prefer a horizontal FlatList.

props.name: variant
props.description: Background color preset drawn from theme. 'light' = white, 'dark' = gray-900, 'primary' = blue-50, 'surface' = gray-50, 'transparent' = transparent.

props.name: backgroundColor
props.description: Custom background color. Overrides variant when supplied.

props.name: padded
props.description: Applies symmetric horizontal and vertical padding to contentContainerStyle. small=12, medium=16, large=24.

props.name: showsVerticalScrollIndicator
props.description: Controls visibility of the vertical scroll bar.

props.name: showsHorizontalScrollIndicator
props.description: Controls visibility of the horizontal scroll bar.

props.name: scrollEnabled
props.description: Enable or disable scrolling. Useful for modal lock patterns.

props.name: flex
props.description: Flex value applied to the scroll container.

props.name: accessibilityLabel
props.description: Accessibility label for screen readers.

props.name: contentContainerStyle
props.description: Style applied to the inner content container. Merged with the padded derived values.

variants.name: light
variants.description: White background — default, suitable for most screens.

variants.name: dark
variants.description: Gray-900 background for dark-mode or night-mode screens.

variants.name: primary
variants.description: Blue-50 background for branded or highlighted scroll areas.

variants.name: surface
variants.description: Gray-50 background for elevated surface screens.

variants.name: transparent
variants.description: No background — allow parent background to show through.

notes: Background colors come directly from theme.colors. The padded prop maps to theme spacing conventions.

examples.title: Basic screen scroll container
examples.description: Wrap a screen's content in StyledScrollView for vertical scrollability.
examples.code:
import { StyledScrollView, YStack, StyledText } from 'fluent-styles';

<StyledScrollView padded="medium">
  <YStack gap={16}>
    <StyledText>Item 1</StyledText>
    <StyledText>Item 2</StyledText>
  </YStack>
</StyledScrollView>

examples.title: Surface-tinted scroll area
examples.description: Use variant='surface' for a card-inset or panel feel.

examples.title: Horizontal scroll row (carousel)
examples.description: Use HorizontalScrollView for image rows or chip groups.
examples.code:
import { HorizontalScrollView, StyledCard } from 'fluent-styles';

<HorizontalScrollView padded="small">
  <StyledCard width={160} marginRight={12}>Card 1</StyledCard>
  <StyledCard width={160} marginRight={12}>Card 2</StyledCard>
  <StyledCard width={160}>Card 3</StyledCard>
</HorizontalScrollView>

bestPractices: Always use padded prop instead of manual margin/padding wrappers on immediate children.
Pass contentContainerStyle for fine-grained inner layout when padded is insufficient.
Use variant to enforce consistent background theming across screens instead of inline backgroundColor.
Pair with KeyboardAvoidingView (also from fluent-styles) when the scroll view contains form inputs.
antiPatterns: Nesting StyledScrollView inside another scrollable — causes scroll conflict on Android.
Applying flex:0 to StyledScrollView — breaks the flex:1 default and clips content.
Using raw RN ScrollView inside fluent-styles screens — breaks theme and spacing consistency.
generationHints: Wrap every long-form screen content in StyledScrollView padded='medium' — never in raw RN ScrollView.
Use HorizontalScrollView for horizontal card rows or chip/filter strips.
Do not nest StyledScrollView; delegate long lists to FlatList or SectionList for virtualization.
Pair StyledScrollView with StyledSafeAreaView at the root and KeyboardAvoidingView when forms are present.

records.summary: Low-level interactive touch primitive with 6 color variants, 3 sizes, border radius control, press/hover state feedback (opacity and scale), and accessibility built-in. Also exports PressableText, PressableIcon, and PressableGroup for common composition patterns.
whenToUse: Use Pressable as a generic touchable area when StyledButton is too opinionated.
Use PressableText when the pressable area should display a text label.
Use PressableIcon for toolbar icon buttons, action icons, and FABs.
Use PressableGroup for segmented controls, tab bars, or button groups with single-select state.
whenNotToUse: Do not use Pressable for primary form submission — use StyledButton instead.
Do not pass raw onPress to non-interactive View; always use Pressable for touchable semantics.
Do not mix PressableGroup with external single-select state without passing selectedId.

props.name: variant
props.description: Background color variant. 'ghost' renders transparent with gray-100 pressed overlay.

props.name: size
props.description: Controls internal padding. small=8, medium=12, large=16.

props.name: rounded
props.description: Border radius preset. none=0, small=4, medium=8, large=16, full=999.

props.name: opacity
props.description: Applies 0.8 opacity on press and 0.9 on hover when true.

props.name: scale
props.description: Applies scale(0.95) on press and scale(1.02) on hover when true.

props.name: backgroundColor
props.description: Custom background color. Overrides variant background.

props.name: pressedColor
props.description: Custom background color when pressed. Overrides variant pressedColor.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Disables press events and applies 0.5 opacity.

props.name: flex
props.description: Flex value applied to the pressable container.

props.name: onPress
props.description: Callback when the element is pressed.

props.name: onLongPress
props.description: Callback when the element is long-pressed.

props.name: accessibilityLabel
props.description: Accessibility label for screen readers.

props.name: accessibilityHint
props.description: Accessibility hint for screen readers.

variants.name: default
variants.description: Gray-200 background with gray-300 pressed state.

variants.name: primary
variants.description: Blue-500 background with blue-600 pressed state.

variants.name: success
variants.description: Green-500 background with green-600 pressed state.

variants.name: warning
variants.description: Amber-500 background with amber-600 pressed state.

variants.name: danger
variants.description: Red-500 background with red-600 pressed state.

variants.name: ghost
variants.description: Transparent background with gray-100 pressed overlay — for icon or text-only actions.

<Pressable variant="primary" size="medium" onPress={() => console.log('pressed')}>

import { Pressable, StyledText } from 'fluent-styles';

<Pressable variant="default" scale opacity onPress={handlePress}>
  <StyledText>Press and hold</StyledText>
</Pressable>

bestPractices: Always pass accessibilityLabel for icon-only pressables.
Use variant='ghost' for actions embedded inside cards or lists to avoid background clashing.
Use PressableIcon over wrapping a raw icon in Pressable to get correct sizing and rounded defaults.
Use PressableGroup for tab/segment patterns to centralize selection state.
antiPatterns: Wrapping a Pressable in a TouchableOpacity — double touch handling causes visual glitches.
Using Pressable for long lists of tappable rows; use FlatList + Pressable per row instead.
Omitting accessibilityRole — it defaults to 'button' but always verify for non-button use cases.
generationHints: Use Pressable for any custom touchable area that StyledButton's opinionated border/radius doesn't suit.
For icon-only tap targets always use PressableIcon — it handles sizing, rounding, and accessibility defaults.
For row/column tab-like selections export PressableGroup with selectedId bound to local state.
Always pass variant='ghost' for pressables embedded inside StyledCard to avoid background conflict.
Pair scale=true and opacity=true for animated feedback on prominent actions.

records.summary: TouchableOpacity-based styled button primitive with link and selected boolean variants. Designed as a low-level building block for composed button UI — not a full-featured button with label; use Pressable or PressableText for richer interaction patterns. Also exports the unwrapped Button styled component for direct use.
whenToUse: Use StyledButton as the base touchable with border, radius, and background defaults when composing custom button rows or cells.
Use Button directly when you need the raw styled primitive without the forwardRef wrapper.
Use the link variant to render a borderless transparent touch target (e.g. navigation links, text CTAs).
Use the selected variant for tab-like or toggle-button states with cyan highlight.
whenNotToUse: Do not use StyledButton for icon-only buttons — use PressableIcon instead.
Do not expect a built-in label or icon; StyledButton renders its children directly.
Do not use for primary submit buttons with rich label+icon layouts — compose using Pressable + StyledText.

props.name: link
props.description: When true, removes border and background (transparent, borderless). Can also accept [isActive, overrideStyles] tuple for per-instance customization.

props.name: selected
props.description: When true, applies cyan-500 border and background to indicate active/selected state. Accepts [isActive, overrideStyles] tuple.

props.name: children
props.description: Content rendered inside the button. Typically StyledText or icons.

variants.name: default
variants.description: Gray-300 background with gray-100 border and 30 border radius. Default row-flex, centered.

variants.name: link
variants.description: Fully transparent, no border, no border radius. Used for text-link style CTAs.

variants.name: selected
variants.description: Cyan-500 background and border. Used for active/toggle states.

notes: Base styles are hardcoded in the styled() call. Variant overrides come from ButtonVariants.
theme.colors.transparent05 is used for the link variant border and background.

import { StyledButton, StyledText } from 'fluent-styles';

<StyledButton onPress={handleSubmit} paddingHorizontal={24} paddingVertical={12}>
  <StyledText fontWeight="bold" color="#fff">Submit</StyledText>
</StyledButton>

examples.description: Renders a transparent, borderless pressable — ideal for text links.

<StyledButton link onPress={navigateToProfile}>
  <StyledText color="#3b82f6">View Profile</StyledText>
</StyledButton>

examples.description: Highlights the active tab with the selected variant.

import { StyledButton, StyledText } from 'fluent-styles';

<StyledButton selected={activeTab === 'orders'} onPress={() => setActiveTab('orders')}>

examples.title: Link variant with style override
examples.description: Tuple form allows overriding specific link styles.
examples.code:
import { StyledButton, StyledText } from 'fluent-styles';

<StyledButton link={[true, { paddingHorizontal: 0 }]} onPress={handlePress}>
  <StyledText>Forgot password?</StyledText>
</StyledButton>

bestPractices: Pass explicit paddingHorizontal and paddingVertical to StyledButton — it ships without padding defaults.
Always wrap text in StyledText inside StyledButton for typography consistency.
Use link variant for secondary text-link CTAs to avoid background or border rendering.
Prefer selected variant for toggle/tab state over managing backgroundColor directly.
antiPatterns: Using StyledButton as a full-featured named button — it has no default label; compose your own.
Supplying nested Touchables inside StyledButton — causes Android press event conflicts.
Relying on the default borderRadius=30 for all contexts; override per design requirement.
generationHints: Use StyledButton as the outer touchable shell and compose StyledText + StyledIcon inside for labelled buttons.
Use link={true} to produce text-link CTAs without any background or border.
Use selected={activeTab === id} to drive active tab or toggle visual state.
For primary action buttons with rich layouts prefer Pressable or PressableText.
Always supply explicit padding — StyledButton has no padding in its base styles.

records.summary: Invisible layout utility for adding precise spacing between elements. Accepts margin, padding, width, height, flex, and gap props directly. Prefer over inline style spacing on sibling components for cleaner, reusable layout composition.
whenToUse: Use to add fixed vertical or horizontal gaps between components that don't support gap natively.
Use flex={1} to push content to the edges of a row or column (flex spacer pattern).
Use between sections of a form or card to apply consistent spacing without wrapping in extra containers.
whenNotToUse: Do not use StyledSpacer inside Stack/XStack/YStack when the gap prop on the parent achieves the same result.
Do not use for semantic structural layout — it is purely a visual spacer.
Do not render multiple adjacent spacers; consolidate into a single height or marginVertical.

props.name: marginHorizontal
props.description: Horizontal margin applied to both left and right.

props.name: marginVertical
props.description: Vertical margin applied to both top and bottom.

props.name: margin
props.description: Uniform margin on all sides.

props.name: padding
props.description: Uniform padding on all sides.

props.name: paddingHorizontal
props.description: Horizontal padding.

props.name: paddingVertical
props.description: Vertical padding.

props.name: width
props.description: Fixed or percentage width for horizontal spacers.

props.name: height
props.description: Fixed or percentage height for vertical spacers.

props.name: flex
props.description: Flex value. Use flex={1} for a greedy spacer that pushes siblings apart.

props.name: gap
props.description: Gap value (rarely needed on spacer itself; useful when spacer wraps content).

notes: No theme color or typography usage. Pure spacing utility.

examples.title: Fixed vertical gap

import { YStack, StyledSpacer, StyledText } from 'fluent-styles';

<YStack>
  <StyledText>Section A</StyledText>
  <StyledSpacer height={24} />
  <StyledText>Section B</StyledText>
</YStack>

examples.title: Flex spacer — push items to edges

import { XStack, StyledSpacer, StyledText, PressableIcon } from 'fluent-styles';

<XStack alignItems="center">
  <StyledText fontWeight="bold" fontSize={18}>Orders</StyledText>
  <StyledSpacer flex={1} />
  <PressableIcon icon={<FilterIcon />} variant="ghost" onPress={openFilters} />
</XStack>

bestPractices: Prefer gap prop on parent Stack/XStack/YStack for uniform spacing between all children.
Use StyledSpacer height={N} only for one-off spacing that breaks a uniform gap pattern.
Use flex={1} spacer pattern to separate label+action pairs in a row layout.
antiPatterns: Using StyledSpacer inside a parent that already has gap — results in compounded spacing.
Using multiple adjacent StyledSpacers — collapse into a single spacer with the total size.
Replacing StyledSpacer with empty View inline styles — breaks the package-first convention.
generationHints: Use StyledSpacer height={N} for breaks between form sections or list groups.
Use StyledSpacer flex={1} inside XStack to push content to opposite ends (e.g., title + action row).
Prefer parent gap={N} on Stack/XStack/YStack over multiple StyledSpacers for uniform item spacing.
Never use raw View with height for spacing; always use StyledSpacer.

records.summary: Flexible label chip component for status indicators, counts, and category tags. Ships 7 color variants, 3 sizes, optional absolute positioning, and sub-exports: BadgeWithIcon (icon+label), BadgeIcon (icon with overlaid count), CounterBadge (numeric with maxCount truncation), and StatusBadge (presence/availability with colored dot).
whenToUse: Use Badge for status labels, category tags, or type indicators inside cards and list rows.
Use CounterBadge to show unread counts or item totals on an icon or thumbnail.
Use BadgeIcon to overlay a notification count badge on an icon or avatar.
Use StatusBadge for user presence indicators (online, offline, busy, idle, etc.).
Use BadgeWithIcon when the badge needs a leading or trailing icon alongside the label.
whenNotToUse: Do not use Badge as a navigation chip or action button — it renders a Text, not a Touchable.
Do not use CounterBadge for large statistical counts (>999); use StyledText directly.
Do not use BadgeIcon without an icon prop — the component only renders the count overlay when icon is present.

props.name: variant
props.description: Color preset for background and text color.

props.name: size
props.description: Controls font size, padding, and border radius. small=nano/12, medium=micro/16, large=small/20.

props.name: backgroundColor
props.description: Custom background color overriding variant.

props.name: color

props.name: borderColor

props.name: borderRadius
props.description: Custom border radius.

props.name: absolute

props.name: top
props.description: Top offset for absolute-positioned badge.

props.name: right
props.description: Right offset for absolute-positioned badge.

props.name: fontWeight

props.name: fontSize
props.description: Font size override.

props.name: children
props.description: Label content.

variants.name: default
variants.description: Gray-100 background, gray-800 text.

variants.name: primary
variants.description: Blue-500 background, white text.

variants.name: success
variants.description: Green-500 background, white text. Use for active/approved states.

variants.name: warning

variants.name: danger
variants.description: Red-500 background, white text. Use for errors or critical states.

variants.name: info
variants.description: Cyan-500 background, white text. Use for informational labels.

variants.name: outline
variants.description: Transparent background with gray-300 border. Use for subtle secondary tags.

bestPractices: Use the variant that semantically matches the state — success for positive, danger for errors, warning for caution.
Use CounterBadge instead of Badge with a number string for counts — it handles showZero and maxCount.
Use absolute={true} with top/right for notification overlays so the badge floats over its parent.
Keep badge labels concise — 1 to 3 words or a number.
antiPatterns: Using Badge as a pressable chip — wrap in Pressable if tappable behavior is needed.
Hardcoding backgroundColor/color when a semantic variant exists.
Using raw Text with custom styles for status chips — Badge provides consistent sizing and theming.
generationHints: Use Badge variant='success'|'warning'|'danger'|'info' for order/transaction/user status chips in list rows.
Use CounterBadge for notification counts on icons — it handles zero-hiding and maxCount truncation automatically.
Use BadgeIcon to wrap any icon that needs a float-count overlay (notifications bell, cart icon, etc.).
Use StatusBadge for user profile presence — it self-manages the dot color and label from status enum.
Place Badge with absolute={true} and top/right offsets inside a relative-positioned parent container.

records.summary: Single-select and multi-select dropdown menu components. Both support controlled and uncontrolled modes, smart upward-flip positioning when near the bottom of the screen, Android BackHandler integration, a MaterialIcons chevron, and modal-based overlay list. MultiSelectDropdown adds checkbox-style multi-selection and a Select All option.
whenToUse: Use Dropdown for single-option selection from a short-to-medium list of items.
Use MultiSelectDropdown when the user needs to select multiple items from a list.
Use controlled mode (value + onChange) when the selection state is managed by a form or parent component.
Use defaultValue for uncontrolled one-time defaults (e.g., pre-filled edit forms).
whenNotToUse: Do not use Dropdown for lists longer than ~50 items without server-side filtering — the FlatList inside the modal is not virtualized beyond its own scroll.
Do not use for boolean toggles — use Switch or CheckBox instead.
Do not use Dropdown inside another Modal — nested modals behave inconsistently on Android.
Do not use when inline radio buttons would be clearer for 2–4 options.

props.description: Text shown when no option is selected.

props.description: Disables the dropdown trigger and applies 0.5 opacity.

props.description: Maximum height of the options list modal. Also used for smart flip-up calculation.

notes: Trigger and list styles are hardcoded from theme.colors. No theme-variant prop on Dropdown itself.
Requires react-native-vector-icons/MaterialIcons to be installed in the consuming app.

bestPractices: Always type the data array as DropdownOptionItem[] — the library imports this type from fluent-styles.
Use controlled mode (value + onChange) in forms managed by react-hook-form or local reducer state.
Set maxHeight to control the visible option list height and prevent the menu from overflowing the screen.
Ensure react-native-vector-icons is linked in the consuming app — Dropdown depends on MaterialIcons.
antiPatterns: Passing value as DropdownOptionItem.value (id string) — it expects DropdownOptionItem.label.
Assuming onChange in MultiSelectDropdown returns a single item — it returns DropdownOptionItem[].
Nesting Dropdown inside a ScrollView that also handles vertical gestures — can cause scroll event conflicts on Android.
Using Dropdown for 2-option boolean choices — Switch or two Pressable buttons are clearer.
generationHints: Use Dropdown for single-value form fields — category picker, sort order, status filter.
Use MultiSelectDropdown for filter panels where multiple values can be selected simultaneously.
Always import DropdownOptionItem type and type the data array — avoids runtime shape mismatches.
Wrap Dropdown in a YStack with a StyledText label above for accessible form field layout.
Pass value + onChange for controlled mode; use defaultValue only for pre-filled display with no external state.

records.summary: Screen header family consisting of StyledHeader (status-bar-aware container), Header (title + back icon row), and Full (passthrough wrapper). Handles iOS/Android status bar height automatically.

records.summary: Circular radio button input for single-select groups. Uses name/selected pattern for group membership. Customizable colors, size, and indicator icon. RadioGroupProps type supports group management.
whenToUse: Use RadioButton for mutually exclusive single-select form options.
Use when users must pick exactly one option from a fixed set.
Use for preference settings, payment methods, or plan selection.
whenNotToUse: Do not use RadioButton for independent toggles; use CheckBox instead.
Do not use when more than 5-6 options exist; consider a Dropdown.

props.name: name
props.description: Unique identifier for this radio button within a group.

props.name: selected
props.description: The currently selected radio button name in the group. RadioButton is selected when selected === name.

props.name: onSelect
props.description: Callback fired with the name of this button when pressed.

props.name: selectedColor
props.description: Background and border color when selected.

props.name: unselectedColor
props.description: Background color when unselected.

props.name: borderColor
props.description: Border color override.

props.name: indicatorColor
props.description: Color of the inner indicator dot.

props.name: size
props.description: Width and height of the radio button in pixels.

props.name: indicatorSize
props.description: Size of the inner indicator icon.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Disables interaction and reduces opacity.

props.name: iconName
props.description: MaterialIcons icon name used as the selection indicator.

variants.name: unselected
variants.description: Gray[50] background, gray[300] border, no indicator.

variants.name: selected
variants.description: Blue[500] background and border, white circle indicator.

variants.name: disabled
variants.description: Opacity 0.6, gray[100] background, interaction blocked.

const [plan, setPlan] = useState('monthly');

<XStack gap={16}>

    <RadioButton name="monthly" selected={plan} onSelect={setPlan} />

    <RadioButton name="annual" selected={plan} onSelect={setPlan} />

  </XStack>
</XStack>

records.summary: Toggle switch family: Switch (bare toggle), SwitchRow (labeled toggle with description), GroupedSwitch (bordered list of labeled switches). Supports size, variant, and controlled/uncontrolled state.
whenToUse: Use Switch for immediate on/off toggles (notifications, settings flags).
Use SwitchRow when each toggle needs a label and optional description.
Use GroupedSwitch for settings screens with multiple labeled toggles in a bordered group.
Use when the toggle effect is immediate (no submit needed).
whenNotToUse: Do not use Switch for multi-select options; use CheckBox.
Do not use when the toggle requires confirmation before applying; use a Dialog.

props.name: value
props.description: Current toggle state.

props.name: onValueChange
props.description: Callback fired with the new boolean state when toggled.

props.name: size
props.description: Scale of the switch. small=1.1x, medium=1.5x, large=2x native scale.

props.name: variant
props.description: Color variant for the on-state track and thumb. primary=blue[400/600], success=green, warning=amber, danger=red.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Disables interaction and reduces opacity to 0.5.

props.name: trackColorOff
props.description: Track color in off state.

props.name: trackColorOn
props.description: Track color in on state. Defaults to variant track color.

props.name: thumbColorOff
props.description: Thumb color in off state.

props.name: thumbColorOn
props.description: Thumb color in on state. Defaults to variant thumb color.

props.name: iosBackgroundColor
props.description: iOS-specific background color.

props.name: label
props.description: SwitchRow only: Label text rendered to the left of the toggle.

props.name: description
props.description: SwitchRow only: Secondary description text below the label.

props.name: switches
props.type: Array<{ id: string; label: string; description?: string; value: boolean }>
props.description: GroupedSwitch only: Array of switch configuration objects.

props.name: onChange
props.description: GroupedSwitch only: Callback fired with the changed switch id and new value.

variants.name: primary
variants.description: Blue[400] track on, blue[600] thumb (default).

variants.name: success
variants.description: Green[400] track on, green[600] thumb.

variants.description: Amber[400] track on, amber[600] thumb.

bestPractices: Use SwitchRow for all labeled toggles to ensure consistent spacing.
Use GroupedSwitch for settings sections with 2+ related toggles.

antiPatterns: Do not use Switch for form options that need explicit submission; use CheckBox.

records.summary: Visual divider family: Separator (line divider), SeparatorWithLabel (divider with centered text), SeparatorGroup (repeated dividers), DottedSeparator (dotted line). Supports horizontal/vertical orientation and four color variants.
whenToUse: Use Separator to visually divide sections or list items.
Use SeparatorWithLabel for 'OR' dividers in login/signup forms.
Use SeparatorGroup for decorative repeated divider patterns.
Use DottedSeparator for decorative or draft-style section breaks.
whenNotToUse: Do not use Separator as a spacer; use StyledSpacer for spacing.
Do not use a full-width Separator inside a card if the card already has dividers.

records.summary: Loading indicator family: Spinner (activity indicator), SpinnerContainer (full-screen modal overlay), InlineSpinner (compact horizontal/vertical spinner with text). Five color variants and three sizes.
whenToUse: Use Spinner for inline loading states within a UI region.
Use SpinnerContainer for full-screen loading overlays (blocking operations).
Use InlineSpinner for compact spinner+text combos inside buttons or list items.
Use overlay={true} on Spinner for a semi-transparent overlay without the SpinnerContainer modal.
whenNotToUse: Do not use SpinnerContainer if the operation is non-blocking; use Spinner.
Do not use Spinner without visual context; always pair with a label or message for accessibility.

props.name: size
props.description: Spinner size. Named: small=30, medium=50, large=80. Accepts raw number.

props.name: variant
props.description: Color variant. primary=blue[500], success=green[500], warning=amber[500], danger=red[500], default=gray[400].

props.name: color
props.description: Override spinner color directly.

props.name: overlay
props.description: Spinner only: When true, renders as a position:absolute semi-transparent overlay.

props.description: Background color of the overlay.

props.name: labelColor
props.description: Color of the label text (white[500] when overlay=true).

props.name: labelSize
props.description: Font size of the label.

props.name: accessibilityLabel
props.description: Accessibility label for screen readers.

props.name: isVisible
props.description: SpinnerContainer only: When false, renders nothing.

props.name: backdropColor

props.name: message

props.name: onBackdropPress

props.name: text
props.description: InlineSpinner only: Text displayed next to the spinner.

props.name: direction
props.description: InlineSpinner only: Layout direction for spinner + text.

props.name: gap
props.description: InlineSpinner only: Gap between spinner and text.

bestPractices: Use SpinnerContainer for blocking operations that prevent user interaction.
Always set isVisible to hide SpinnerContainer when loading is done.
Use label or message for all spinners to improve accessibility.
Use InlineSpinner with direction='row' for button loading states.
antiPatterns: Do not use SpinnerContainer for non-blocking operations; use Spinner.
Do not omit label/message — provide context for screen readers and users.
generationHints: Import Spinner, SpinnerContainer, or InlineSpinner from 'fluent-styles'.
Use SpinnerContainer for full-screen loading overlays controlled by isVisible.
Use Spinner with overlay={true} for in-screen partial overlays.
Use InlineSpinner for compact loading states inline with other UI.
Default variant is 'primary' (blue); use 'success' after completion feedback.
